STORAGE.name: Data Storage
STORAGE.description: Secure storage of sensitive data on the device.

CRYPTO.name: Cryptography
CRYPTO.description: Use of cryptography to protect sensitive data.

PLATFORM.name: Platform Interaction
PLATFORM.description: Secure use of platform APIs and components.

CODE.name: Code Quality
CODE.description: Security best practices for code quality.

findings.title: Hardcoded API Keys/Secrets
findings.description: API keys, passwords, or secrets hardcoded directly in source code. Attackers can extract these through reverse engineering or by accessing the source repository.
findings.remediation: Store secrets in secure storage (flutter_secure_storage), environment variables, or a secrets management service. Never commit credentials to source control.

findings.title: Sensitive Data in SharedPreferences
findings.description: Storing sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII) in SharedPreferences without encryption. SharedPreferences data is stored in plaintext XML files accessible on rooted devices.
findings.remediation: Use flutter_secure_storage for sensitive data, which encrypts data using platform-specific secure storage (Keychain on iOS, EncryptedSharedPreferences on Android).

findings.title: Sensitive Data in SharedPreferences

findings.title: Logging Sensitive Data
findings.description: Printing sensitive information (passwords, tokens, PII) to logs using print(), debugPrint(), or logging frameworks. Logs may be accessible through ADB or crash reports.
findings.remediation: Remove sensitive data from logs. Use conditional logging that is disabled in release builds. Implement log sanitization.

findings.title: Logging Sensitive Data

findings.remediation: Remove sensitive data from logs. Use conditional logging that is disabled in release builds. Implement log sanitization.

findings.title: Sensitive Data in Clipboard
findings.description: Copying sensitive data to the system clipboard. Other apps can access clipboard content, and it may persist after the app is closed.
findings.remediation: Avoid copying sensitive data to clipboard. If necessary, clear clipboard after a timeout. Warn users before copying sensitive data.

findings.title: Sensitive Data in Clipboard
findings.description: Copying sensitive data to the system clipboard. Other apps can access clipboard content, and it may persist after the app is closed.
findings.remediation: Avoid copying sensitive data to clipboard. If necessary, clear clipboard after a timeout. Warn users before copying sensitive data.

findings.title: Sensitive Data in Clipboard
findings.description: Copying sensitive data to the system clipboard. Other apps can access clipboard content, and it may persist after the app is closed.
findings.remediation: Avoid copying sensitive data to clipboard. If necessary, clear clipboard after a timeout. Warn users before copying sensitive data.

findings.title: Sensitive Data in Clipboard
findings.description: Copying sensitive data to the system clipboard. Other apps can access clipboard content, and it may persist after the app is closed.
findings.remediation: Avoid copying sensitive data to clipboard. If necessary, clear clipboard after a timeout. Warn users before copying sensitive data.

findings.title: Weak Hashing Algorithms (MD5/SHA1)
findings.description: Using deprecated or weak hashing algorithms like MD5 or SHA1. These algorithms have known vulnerabilities and should not be used for security-sensitive operations.
findings.remediation: Use strong hashing algorithms: SHA-256, SHA-3, or bcrypt/Argon2 for password hashing. MD5/SHA1 are only acceptable for non-security checksums.

findings.title: Weak Hashing Algorithms (MD5/SHA1)
findings.description: Using deprecated or weak hashing algorithms like MD5 or SHA1. These algorithms have known vulnerabilities and should not be used for security-sensitive operations.
findings.remediation: Use strong hashing algorithms: SHA-256, SHA-3, or bcrypt/Argon2 for password hashing. MD5/SHA1 are only acceptable for non-security checksums.

findings.title: Hardcoded Encryption Keys
findings.description: Encryption keys hardcoded in source code. Compromises all encrypted data if source code is exposed through reverse engineering or repository access.
findings.remediation: Generate keys dynamically, store in secure storage (flutter_secure_storage), or use a key management service. Derive keys from user credentials using PBKDF2/Argon2.

findings.title: Hardcoded Encryption Keys
findings.description: Encryption keys hardcoded in source code. Compromises all encrypted data if source code is exposed through reverse engineering or repository access.
findings.remediation: Generate keys dynamically, store in secure storage (flutter_secure_storage), or use a key management service. Derive keys from user credentials using PBKDF2/Argon2.

findings.title: Hardcoded Encryption Keys
findings.description: Encryption keys hardcoded in source code. Compromises all encrypted data if source code is exposed through reverse engineering or repository access.
findings.remediation: Generate keys dynamically, store in secure storage (flutter_secure_storage), or use a key management service. Derive keys from user credentials using PBKDF2/Argon2.

findings.title: Hardcoded Encryption Keys
findings.description: Encryption keys hardcoded in source code. Compromises all encrypted data if source code is exposed through reverse engineering or repository access.
findings.remediation: Generate keys dynamically, store in secure storage (flutter_secure_storage), or use a key management service. Derive keys from user credentials using PBKDF2/Argon2.

findings.title: Insecure Random Number Generation
findings.description: Using non-cryptographic random number generators for security-sensitive operations like token generation, nonces, or key generation.
findings.remediation: Use Random.secure() for cryptographically secure random numbers in Dart. Never use math.Random() for security-sensitive operations.

findings.title: Insecure Random Number Generation
findings.description: Using non-cryptographic random number generators for security-sensitive operations like token generation, nonces, or key generation.
findings.remediation: Use Random.secure() for cryptographically secure random numbers in Dart. Never use math.Random() for security-sensitive operations.

findings.title: Insecure Biometric Authentication
findings.description: Biometric authentication that only checks a boolean result without cryptographic binding. Attackers can bypass this with function hooking on rooted devices.
findings.remediation: Use biometrics with cryptographic binding. Store sensitive keys in the Keystore/Keychain and require biometric authentication to access them.

findings.title: Insecure Biometric Authentication
findings.description: Biometric authentication that only checks a boolean result without cryptographic binding. Attackers can bypass this with function hooking on rooted devices.
findings.remediation: Use biometrics with cryptographic binding. Store sensitive keys in the Keystore/Keychain and require biometric authentication to access them.

findings.title: Missing Session Timeout
findings.description: App does not implement session timeout, allowing indefinite session validity. Compromised tokens remain valid indefinitely.
findings.remediation: Implement session timeout with automatic logout. Use token expiration and refresh tokens. Consider idle timeout based on user inactivity.

findings.title: Weak Local PIN/Password Policy
findings.description: App allows weak PINs (e.g., 4 digits, no complexity requirements) or passwords without enforcing strength requirements.
findings.remediation: Enforce strong PIN/password policy: minimum 6 digits for PIN, 8+ characters with complexity for passwords. Block common PINs (1234, 0000) and weak passwords.

findings.title: Weak Local PIN/Password Policy
findings.description: App allows weak PINs (e.g., 4 digits, no complexity requirements) or passwords without enforcing strength requirements.
findings.remediation: Enforce strong PIN/password policy: minimum 6 digits for PIN, 8+ characters with complexity for passwords. Block common PINs (1234, 0000) and weak passwords.

findings.title: Insecure HTTP Connections
findings.description: Using unencrypted HTTP instead of HTTPS for network communication. Data transmitted in plaintext can be intercepted by attackers on the same network.
findings.remediation: Always use HTTPS for network communication. Configure Network Security Config on Android to block cleartext traffic. Use http package with https:// URLs only.

findings.title: Insecure HTTP Connections
findings.description: Using unencrypted HTTP instead of HTTPS for network communication. Data transmitted in plaintext can be intercepted by attackers on the same network.
findings.remediation: Always use HTTPS for network communication. Configure Network Security Config on Android to block cleartext traffic. Use http package with https:// URLs only.

findings.title: Insecure HTTP Connections
findings.description: Using unencrypted HTTP instead of HTTPS for network communication. Data transmitted in plaintext can be intercepted by attackers on the same network.
findings.remediation: Always use HTTPS for network communication. Configure Network Security Config on Android to block cleartext traffic. Use http package with https:// URLs only.

findings.title: Insecure HTTP Connections
findings.description: Using unencrypted HTTP instead of HTTPS for network communication. Data transmitted in plaintext can be intercepted by attackers on the same network.
findings.remediation: Always use HTTPS for network communication. Configure Network Security Config on Android to block cleartext traffic. Use http package with https:// URLs only.

findings.title: Insecure HTTP Connections
findings.description: Using unencrypted HTTP instead of HTTPS for network communication. Data transmitted in plaintext can be intercepted by attackers on the same network.
findings.remediation: Always use HTTPS for network communication. Configure Network Security Config on Android to block cleartext traffic. Use http package with https:// URLs only.

findings.title: Disabled SSL/TLS Certificate Validation
findings.description: Disabling or bypassing SSL certificate validation allows man-in-the-middle attacks. Attackers can intercept and modify all network traffic.
findings.remediation: Never disable certificate validation in production. Use certificate pinning for additional security. Only bypass for development with proper safeguards and kDebugMode checks.

findings.title: Disabled SSL/TLS Certificate Validation
findings.description: Disabling or bypassing SSL certificate validation allows man-in-the-middle attacks. Attackers can intercept and modify all network traffic.
findings.remediation: Never disable certificate validation in production. Use certificate pinning for additional security. Only bypass for development with proper safeguards and kDebugMode checks.

findings.title: Disabled SSL/TLS Certificate Validation
findings.description: Disabling or bypassing SSL certificate validation allows man-in-the-middle attacks. Attackers can intercept and modify all network traffic.
findings.remediation: Never disable certificate validation in production. Use certificate pinning for additional security. Only bypass for development with proper safeguards and kDebugMode checks.

findings.title: Missing Certificate Pinning
findings.description: App does not implement certificate pinning, allowing attackers with CA-signed certificates to perform man-in-the-middle attacks.
findings.remediation: Implement certificate pinning using packages like http_certificate_pinning or dio_http2_adapter. Pin to leaf certificate or public key hash.

findings.title: Missing Certificate Pinning
findings.description: App does not implement certificate pinning, allowing attackers with CA-signed certificates to perform man-in-the-middle attacks.
findings.remediation: Implement certificate pinning using packages like http_certificate_pinning or dio_http2_adapter. Pin to leaf certificate or public key hash.

findings.title: Missing Certificate Pinning
findings.description: App does not implement certificate pinning, allowing attackers with CA-signed certificates to perform man-in-the-middle attacks.
findings.remediation: Implement certificate pinning using packages like http_certificate_pinning or dio_http2_adapter. Pin to leaf certificate or public key hash.

findings.title: Insecure Deep Link Handling
findings.description: Deep links that don't validate input before processing sensitive operations. Attackers can craft malicious deep links to trigger unintended actions.
findings.remediation: Validate all deep link parameters. Implement allowlist for valid deep link paths. Require authentication for sensitive deep link actions.

findings.title: Insecure Deep Link Handling
findings.description: Deep links that don't validate input before processing sensitive operations. Attackers can craft malicious deep links to trigger unintended actions.
findings.remediation: Validate all deep link parameters. Implement allowlist for valid deep link paths. Require authentication for sensitive deep link actions.

findings.title: WebView with JavaScript Enabled
findings.description: WebView components with JavaScript enabled loading untrusted content. JavaScript can be used for XSS attacks or to access sensitive WebView APIs.
findings.remediation: Disable JavaScript unless strictly required. Only load trusted content. Implement Content Security Policy. Validate and sanitize all URLs before loading.

findings.title: WebView with JavaScript Enabled
findings.description: WebView components with JavaScript enabled loading untrusted content. JavaScript can be used for XSS attacks or to access sensitive WebView APIs.
findings.remediation: Disable JavaScript unless strictly required. Only load trusted content. Implement Content Security Policy. Validate and sanitize all URLs before loading.

findings.title: WebView with JavaScript Enabled
findings.description: WebView components with JavaScript enabled loading untrusted content. JavaScript can be used for XSS attacks or to access sensitive WebView APIs.

 273           initialUrl: 'https://untrusted-site.com',
 274           javascriptMode: JavascriptMode.unrestricted,

findings.remediation: Disable JavaScript unless strictly required. Only load trusted content. Implement Content Security Policy. Validate and sanitize all URLs before loading.

findings.title: Sensitive UI Not Protected from Screenshots
findings.description: Screens displaying sensitive data (passwords, financial info) are not protected from screenshots or screen recording. Data may leak through screenshots or recent apps view.

location.code_snippet:
312           body: Column(
 313             children: [
 314 >>>           TextField(
 315                 obscureText: true,
 316                 decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Password'),

findings.remediation: Use flutter_windowmanager or platform channels to set FLAG_SECURE on Android. Use appropriate UIScreen settings on iOS. Implement screen capture detection.

findings.title: Debug Mode Enabled in Production
findings.description: Debug flags or assertions left enabled in production builds. Exposes sensitive debugging information and may enable hidden functionality.
findings.remediation: Remove all debug code before release. Use kDebugMode/kReleaseMode flags to conditionally execute debug code. Verify build configuration excludes debug features.

findings.title: Debug Mode Enabled in Production
findings.description: Debug flags or assertions left enabled in production builds. Exposes sensitive debugging information and may enable hidden functionality.
findings.remediation: Remove all debug code before release. Use kDebugMode/kReleaseMode flags to conditionally execute debug code. Verify build configuration excludes debug features.

findings.title: Debug Mode Enabled in Production
findings.description: Debug flags or assertions left enabled in production builds. Exposes sensitive debugging information and may enable hidden functionality.
findings.remediation: Remove all debug code before release. Use kDebugMode/kReleaseMode flags to conditionally execute debug code. Verify build configuration excludes debug features.

findings.title: Debug Mode Enabled in Production
findings.description: Debug flags or assertions left enabled in production builds. Exposes sensitive debugging information and may enable hidden functionality.
findings.remediation: Remove all debug code before release. Use kDebugMode/kReleaseMode flags to conditionally execute debug code. Verify build configuration excludes debug features.

findings.title: Debug Mode Enabled in Production
findings.description: Debug flags or assertions left enabled in production builds. Exposes sensitive debugging information and may enable hidden functionality.
findings.remediation: Remove all debug code before release. Use kDebugMode/kReleaseMode flags to conditionally execute debug code. Verify build configuration excludes debug features.

findings.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
findings.description: Building SQL queries through string concatenation with user input allows attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands.
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements. Never concatenate user input into SQL strings. Use ORM libraries like Drift (formerly Moor).

findings.title: Missing Root/Jailbreak Detection
findings.description: App does not detect if running on a rooted/jailbroken device. Rooted devices can bypass app security controls and access protected data.
findings.remediation: Implement root/jailbreak detection using flutter_jailbreak_detection, root_checker, or safe_device packages. Take appropriate action (warn user, limit functionality, or exit) when detected.

findings.title: Missing App Integrity Verification
findings.description: App does not verify its own integrity, allowing modified or repackaged versions to run. Attackers can inject malicious code.
findings.remediation: Implement app integrity checking using Play Integrity API (Android) or App Attest (iOS). Verify app signature at runtime. Use code signing verification.

findings.title: Missing App Integrity Verification
findings.description: App does not verify its own integrity, allowing modified or repackaged versions to run. Attackers can inject malicious code.
findings.remediation: Implement app integrity checking using Play Integrity API (Android) or App Attest (iOS). Verify app signature at runtime. Use code signing verification.

findings.title: Missing App Integrity Verification
findings.description: App does not verify its own integrity, allowing modified or repackaged versions to run. Attackers can inject malicious code.
findings.remediation: Implement app integrity checking using Play Integrity API (Android) or App Attest (iOS). Verify app signature at runtime. Use code signing verification.

findings.title: Excessive Permission Requests
findings.description: App requests more permissions than necessary for its functionality, violating the principle of least privilege and potentially exposing user data.
findings.remediation: Review and minimize required permissions. Request permissions at runtime only when needed. Clearly explain to users why each permission is required.

findings.remediation: Review and minimize required permissions. Request permissions at runtime only when needed. Clearly explain to users why each permission is required.

findings.title: Collection of Persistent Device Identifiers
findings.description: App collects hardware identifiers (IMEI, MAC address, Android ID) that can track users across app reinstalls. This raises privacy concerns and may violate regulations.
findings.remediation: Avoid collecting persistent device identifiers. Use instance-specific identifiers that reset on reinstall. For advertising, use the platform advertising ID with user consent.

findings.remediation: Avoid collecting persistent device identifiers. Use instance-specific identifiers that reset on reinstall. For advertising, use the platform advertising ID with user consent.
